danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened/charred magical candy item in castle garden
danger_signals.meaning: Sign of external threat; monsters are destroying light sources; indicates corrupting influence affecting the kingdom

danger_signals.visual_form: Queen Velvet speaks nonsensically and rides away on Pinky without warning
danger_signals.meaning: World-state corruption spreading; madness as a contagion vector; loss of stable leadership

danger_signals.visual_form: Oncoming enemies in pseudo-3D scrolling perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zone; burning Willows indicates deliberate sabotage/hostility

safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; sustaining world light and stability; player progression security

safety_signals.visual_form: Castle garden, Silk/Knit NPCs, Fairy Queen's court
safety_signals.meaning: Established order; protection framework; organized response to chaos

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle garden, organized leadership structure, light-bringing magic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse oncoming enemy sprites in pseudo-3D space
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Northern regions; active destruction of light sources; chaotic advancement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Appetite for Willows; magical spellcasting ability
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mobile protagonist; external to kingdom hierarchy; reactive enforcement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle garden contains Willows; kingdom operates normally with Queen Velvet in command
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Silk and Knit manage routine fairy duties; no urgent threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Burnt Willow appears in castle garden; Queen Velvet exhibits madness and rides away; monsters approach from north
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Queen's erratic decision-making and abandonment of post; Silk and Knit shift to crisis response; Cotton summoned into action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Northern monsters continuously burning Willows; pseudo-3D enemies scroll toward player 'into screen'
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protagonist moves across screen shooting and avoiding obstacles; weapon upgrades collected; spell deployment required

environmental_storytelling.example: Burnt Willow in castle garden immediately signals external threat; its corrupting effect on Queen Velvet demonstrates danger propagation mechanism
Queen Velvet's coherent leadership → nonsensical speech → abandonment of post shows psychological/magical corruption progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Scrolling enemy approach as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Pseudo-3D perspective with enemies advancing 'into screen' mimics invasion frontline; density and type variation indicate regional threat levels

environmental_storytelling.technique: Light-source destruction as world degradation metaphor
environmental_storytelling.example: Willows = light = order; burnt Willows = darkness = chaos; systematic destruction of Willows represents methodical world-destabilization

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Speaks incoherently; believes she alone can save world; abandons throne and rides away on Pinky; unreachable by other fairies
Shifts from routine duties to investigation; attempts to isolate threat (burnt Willow); recruits Cotton; leads adventure
Collaborates with Silk to diagnose problem; participates in threat assessment
Appears spontaneously; eats burnt Willow (immune or testing?); spits it out in disgust; immediately commits to stopping Willow destruction; joins Silk as active combatant
Burn Willows systematically; approach from north in organized waves; respond to player fire; demonstrate varied attack patterns and movement

notes: Panorama Cotton uses a narrative-gameplay fusion where visual world-state corruption (burnt Willow artifact, Queen's madness, northern invasion) directly triggers the pseudo-3D scrolling-shooter gameplay mechanics. The game communicates political collapse through character behavioral tells (Queen abandoning post) and resource destruction (Willows burnt). The pseudo-3D perspective itself becomes a manifestation layer: enemies advancing 'into screen' visualizes the threat vector and invasion frontline. Light metaphor (Willows = illumination = order) underpins both plot and mechanical progression. No explicit faction colors or sigils documented; faction identity relies on behavioral patterns, artifact associations, and environmental positioning.
